Quercus refers to the genus of oak trees, which are deciduous or evergreen trees or shrubs belonging to the beech family. The health benefits of Quercus include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its use in traditional medicine for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Quercus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which have been shown to have potential health benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with Quercus include oak bark tea, oak leaf tea, and oak extract supplements, which are used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and other health conditions.
